categories.name: Academic Writing
categories.description: Rules specific to scholarly, research, and academic prose.

rules.title: First person in academic writing
rules.pattern_description: Flag 'I think', 'I believe', 'I feel', 'In my opinion' in academic register

rules.title: Contractions in academic writing
rules.pattern_description: Flag don't, can't, won't, it's, they're etc. in formal academic text

rules.title: Vague quantifiers
rules.pattern_description: Flag 'a lot of', 'lots of', 'tons of', 'a bunch of' â€” prefer precise data

rules.title: Weasel words
rules.pattern_description: some people say, it is believed, studies show, experts say, research suggests â€” cite specifically

rules.title: Rhetorical questions in papers
rules.pattern_description: Questions in academic papers should generally be avoided

rules.title: Informal transitions
rules.pattern_description: Plus, Also at sentence start, So, Anyway, Well â€” use Moreover, Furthermore, Additionally

categories.name: Business Writing
categories.description: Rules for professional emails, reports, memos.

rules.title: Passive in instructions
rules.pattern_description: In instructions, prefer active: 'Submit the report' not 'The report should be submitted'

rules.title: Empty openers
rules.pattern_description: I am writing to inform you, This email is to let you know, I wanted to reach out â€” get to the point

rules.title: Weak verbs
rules.pattern_description: utilizeâ†’use, implementâ†’do/start, facilitateâ†’help, commenceâ†’begin/start, terminateâ†’end, endeavorâ†’try

rules.title: Double negatives for politeness
rules.pattern_description: 'not uncommon'â†’'common', 'not unlike'â†’'similar to', 'not insignificant'â†’'significant'

rules.title: Overly complex words
rules.pattern_description: ameliorateâ†’improve, ascertainâ†’find out, cognizantâ†’aware, elucidateâ†’explain, expediteâ†’speed up, juxtaposeâ†’compare, paradigmâ†’model, remunerationâ†’pay, ubiquitousâ†’widespread, veraciousâ†’truthful

rules.title: Gendered job titles
rules.pattern_description: chairmanâ†’chairperson/chair, firemanâ†’firefighter, policemanâ†’police officer, stewardessâ†’flight attendant, mailmanâ†’mail carrier, mankindâ†’humankind, manpowerâ†’workforce, man-madeâ†’artificial/synthetic

rules.title: Generic 'he'
rules.pattern_description: 'When a student submits his work' â†’ 'their work' or 'his or her work'

rules.title: Person-first language
rules.pattern_description: 'disabled person'â†’'person with a disability', 'autistic child'â†’'child with autism', 'blind person'â†’'person who is blind'

rules.title: Ableist language
rules.pattern_description: 'falling on deaf ears', 'blind spot' (metaphorical), 'lame excuse', 'crippled by', 'suffers from', 'confined to a wheelchair'â†’'uses a wheelchair'

rules.title: Age-related language
rules.pattern_description: 'elderly'â†’'older adults', 'senior citizens'â†’'older adults'

categories.name: Readability
categories.description: Sentence complexity, reading level, clarity metrics.

rules.title: Very long sentences
rules.pattern_description: Flag sentences over 40 words â€” suggest breaking up

rules.title: Long paragraphs
rules.pattern_description: Flag paragraphs over 150 words

rules.title: Complex word alternatives
rules.pattern_description: Suggest simpler synonyms: utilizeâ†’use, demonstrateâ†’show, approximatelyâ†’about, subsequentâ†’next, priorâ†’before, commenceâ†’start, terminateâ†’end, sufficientâ†’enough, endeavorâ†’try, neverthelessâ†’still, notwithstandingâ†’despite, heretoforeâ†’previously, aforementionedâ†’previous, inasmuchâ†’since, henceforthâ†’from now on, therebyâ†’by this, wherebyâ†’by which, thereinâ†’in that

rules.title: Consecutive prepositional phrases
rules.pattern_description: Flag 3+ prepositional phrases in a row â€” indicates complexity

rules.title: Repeated sentence beginnings
rules.pattern_description: Flag when 3+ consecutive sentences start with the same word
